no_changes_rationale:
status: already_complete
verification_command: ddx doc audit

All three ACs are satisfied by content already present in the codebase at the
base revision (e338ce9e3). A prior execution of this bead authored the required
artifacts and had them merged into main; the bead was never formally closed.

AC 1 — All three FEATs updated:
  FEAT-006 §"Review routing request facts" (lines 78-84): reviewer pairing via
    MinPower bump + role=reviewer + correlation metadata; references ADR-024.
  FEAT-006 §"Escalation And Review Routing Boundary" (lines 213-226):
    MinPower/MaxPower escalation ladder with no Fizeau extension required;
    DDx does not translate the floor into a concrete model/provider.
  FEAT-010 §"Agent Power and Retry": escalation via MinPower raise, sticky
    passthrough constraints, capability-sensitive retry eligibility.
  FEAT-010 §"Cost stop" (step 8 of the decision tree): implementation and
    review attempts both count against the drain-level cost cap.
  FEAT-014 requirement 13a: review cost and pairing signals normalized with
    role=reviewer, review_group_id, reviewer slot, implementer/reviewer route
    facts; review-pairing-degraded is a routing-quality signal for operators,
    not a route-selection input owned by DDx.
  FEAT-014 §"Out of Scope": queue-drain budget stop and reviewer-cost cap are
    owned by FEAT-010 and ADR-024; FEAT-014 supplies the normalized cost signals.

AC 2 — ADR-021 authored:
  docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-024-power-escalation-and-review-routing.md
  exists and was authored by this bead. The bead title used "ADR-021" as a
  working number; ADR-024 was assigned because ADRs 021-023 were already
  occupied. ADR-024 explicitly documents:
    - "These fields are Day-1 observability."
    - "Future agent-side routing intelligence may use Role and correlation
       internally, but DDx does not specify that algorithm and must not
       depend on it."
  Both the Day-1 observability framing and the Fizeau-internal evolution
  clause are present verbatim.

AC 3 — ddx doc audit clean:
  Verified: `ddx doc audit` returns "Document graph is clean."
